content:
Hi! I wish to work on adding Nova support in the NVK Vulkan driver in Mesa. A good portion of that involves replacing the DRM calls from Nouveau to Nova.

Because of that, I want to start by making a test suite that verifies the common DRM calls work as-intended. The baseline would be ensuring the results match from Nvidia's Open GPU Kernel module.

I was wondering if this would be valuable, if feasible. If so, any advice on getting started?
I assume there's already some coverage in IGT, perhaps expanding on that would be the most efficient way forward?
Oh wow, IGT is pretty much what I had in mind. Neat! Thank you @Matt Coster ! I'll look into that.
That would definitely be premature, provided that there are no DRM calls supported by Nova yet

Oh, sorry! I was under the impression that there were at least three DRM calls currently implemented.

If it is too premature, how about this: Whenever a DRM call is implemented, I'll work with the contributor and setup an automated check to ensure that the call continues to work as-intended. That way, the project can know whenever there are regressions.
